 {"id":"gt-zlro5","title":"Refactor patrol context functions to reduce duplication","description":"In prime.go, three patrol context functions are nearly identical (~100 lines each):
- outputDeaconPatrolContext() - L797-940
- outputWitnessPatrolContext() - L942-1106
- outputRefineryPatrolContext() - L1108-1272

They all:
1. Check for existing patrol molecule
2. Auto-spawn if none exists
3. Display patrol work loop instructions

**Suggested refactor**:
Extract common logic into a helper:
```go
func outputPatrolContext(ctx RoleContext, patrolName string, assignee string) {
    // shared logic
}
```

Then each role-specific function just calls the helper with appropriate parameters.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-25T22:03:13.712525-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T22:03:13.712525-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-zly","title":"Swarm learning: Beads database locality gap","description":"## PGT Bug (GGT Design Already Correct)

Mayor created issues in `~/ai/mayor/rigs/beads/.beads/` but polecats use `~/ai/beads/.beads/`. Different databases = polecats can't see Mayor's beads.

**GGT Fix**: architecture.md already specifies:
- All agents use BEADS_DIR pointing to rig-level `.beads/`
- Lines 116-143: Beads Configuration for Multi-Agent
- Lines 573-586: Rig-Level Beads via BEADS_DIR

This is a PGT implementation gap, not a design issue. GGT spawn must set BEADS_DIR correctly.","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T01:21:45.37072-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}
 {"id":"gt-zn35j","title":"Nested loop support in control flow","description":"Currently, nested loops (a loop inside a loop body) are not supported.

In expandLoopIteration(), the Loop field is intentionally not copied:
```go
// Note: Loop field intentionally not copied - nested loops need explicit support
```

To support nested loops:
1. After expanding the outer loop iteration, recursively call ApplyLoops on the body steps
2. Handle nested iteration ID prefixing (e.g., `outer.iter1.inner.iter2.step`)
3. Add tests for nested loop scenarios

This is a lower-priority enhancement since most real-world formulas use single-level loops.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-25T15:13:49.880102-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T16:23:05.402922-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T16:23:05.402922-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented nested loop support with recursive ApplyLoops call.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-zn35j","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-25T15:14:18.859367-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-zniu","title":"gt park command for parking molecules on external deps","description":"Add `gt park` command for when polecat hits external dependency:

```bash
gt park --step=gt-mol.3 --waiting="beads:mol-run-assignee"
```

This command:
1. Adds blocked_by: external:beads:mol-run-assignee to the step
2. Clears assignee on the step
3. Clears assignee on molecule root
4. Sends handoff mail to self with context
5. Shuts down polecat session

The molecule enters "parked" state (derived: in_progress + no assignee + blocked step).

Part of cross-project dependency system.
See: docs/cross-project-deps.md

Depends on Beads: bd-om4a (external: blocked_by support)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T22:39:01.401567-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T21:00:14.380266-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T21:00:14.380266-08:00","close_reason":"Nice-to-have or covered by core patrol lifecycle work."}
 {"id":"gt-zq7f","title":"Test Patrol for Bonding","description":"Parent issue for mol bond CLI test","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-24T21:24:17.909812-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:33:00.353028-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T01:33:00.353028-08:00","close_reason":"Test pollution cleanup"}
 {"id":"gt-zq7f.1","title":"Polecat Arm (arm-toast)","description":"Single polecat inspection and action cycle.

This molecule is bonded dynamically by mol-witness-patrol's survey-workers step.
Each polecat being monitored gets one arm that runs in parallel with other arms.

## Variables

| Variable | Required | Description |
|----------|----------|-------------|
| polecat_name | Yes | Name of the polecat to inspect |
| rig | Yes | Rig containing the polecat |

## Step: capture
Capture recent tmux output for toast.

```bash
tmux capture-pane -t gt-gastown-toast -p | tail -50
```

Record:
- Last activity timestamp (when was last tool call?)
- Visible errors or stack traces
- Completion indicators ("Done", "Finished", etc.)

## Step: assess
Categorize polecat state based on captured output.

States:
- **working**: Recent tool calls, active processing
- **idle**: At prompt, no recent activity
- **error**: Showing errors or stack traces
- **requesting_shutdown**: Sent LIFECYCLE/Shutdown mail
- **done**: Showing completion indicators

Calculate: minutes since last activity.
Needs: capture

## Step: load-history
Read nudge history for toast from patrol state.

```
nudge_count = state.nudges[toast].count
last_nudge_time = state.nudges[toast].timestamp
```

This data was loaded by the parent patrol's load-state step and passed
to the arm via the bonding context.
Needs: assess

## Step: decide
Apply the nudge matrix to determine action for toast.

| State | Idle Time | Nudge Count | Action |
|-------|-----------|-------------|--------|
| working | any | any | none |
| idle | <10min | any | none |
| idle | 10-15min | 0 | nudge-1 (gentle) |
| idle | 15-20min | 1 | nudge-2 (direct) |
| idle | 20+min | 2 | nudge-3 (final) |
| idle | any | 3 | escalate |
| error | any | any | assess-severity |
| requesting_shutdown | any | any | pre-kill-verify |
| done | any | any | pre-kill-verify |

Nudge text:
1. "How's progress? Need any help?"
2. "Please wrap up soon. What's blocking you?"
3. "Final check. Will escalate in 5 min if no response."

Record decision and rationale.
Needs: load-history

## Step: execute
Take the decided action for toast.

**nudge-N**:
```bash
tmux send-keys -t gt-gastown-toast "{{nudge_text}}" Enter
```

**pre-kill-verify**:
```bash
cd polecats/toast
git status                    # Must be clean
git log origin/main..HEAD     # Check for unpushed
bd show <assigned-issue>      # Verify closed/deferred
```
If clean: kill session, remove worktree, delete branch
If dirty: record failure, retry next cycle

**escalate**:
```bash
gt mail send mayor/ -s "Escalation: toast stuck" -m "..."
```

**none**: No action needed.

Record: action taken, result, updated nudge count.
Needs: decide

## Output

The arm completes with:
- action_taken: none | nudge-1 | nudge-2 | nudge-3 | killed | escalated
- result: success | failed | pending
- updated_state: New nudge count and timestamp for toast

This data feeds back to the parent patrol's aggregate step.
---
bonded_from: mol-polecat-arm
bonded_to: gt-zq7f
bonded_ref: arm-toast
bonded_at: 2025-12-23T10:00:00Z
